title: Discovering Magnetic Field Lines
summary: Explore how magnetic fields are visualized through invisible lines of force that reveal the strength and direction around magnets.

sections.heading: What Are Magnetic Field Lines?
sections.body: Magnetic field lines represent the pathway along which a magnetic force acts on moving charges. Imagine an invisible pattern that wraps around magnets, this is their behavior.

sections.heading: How Do Magnetic Field Lines Form?
sections.body: Magnetic field lines originate from the north pole and terminate at the south pole, forming loops. The closer these invisible strands are to each other, the stronger the magnetic force in that area.

sections.heading: Why Are Magnetic Field Lines Important?
sections.body: Understanding magnetic field lines helps us visualize the invisible force that affects compass needles, plays a role in Earth's protection from solar winds and is essential for understanding magnetism itself.

key_facts: The magnetic field lines always close back to the starting point, forming closed loops.
